vulnerability.title: Verify Permissions on shadow File
vulnerability.description: To properly set the permissions of /etc/shadow, run the command: $ sudo chmod 0000 /etc/shadow

triage_decision.reason: Adjusting /etc/shadow permissions to the STIG‑specified mode is a standard hardening step. Root retains access via privileged operations, and the change does not affect service start‑up or network connectivity, making it low risk for lockout.
triage_decision.estimated_impact: authentication/authorization affected; Incorrect ownership could prevent authentication services from reading the file; Applying overly restrictive bits might interfere with processes that run as the shadow group
